ucs.title: [LLM] TA578 Latrodectus/IcedID/Bumblebee loader: msiexec remote-URL install chained to rundll32 export-name DLL load
ucs.description: Hunts TA578's signature loader chain — a JavaScript / browser-spawned msiexec.exe pulling an MSI directly over HTTPS, followed within minutes by rundll32.exe loading a DLL from %AppData% with a named export (e.g. Latrodectus 'AlexolTon' / 'Run' or IcedID 'PluginInit' / 'DllRegisterServer'). This MSI-to-rundll32 hand-off is the consistent fingerprint Proofpoint/Bitsight have attributed to TA578 since the IcedID era through Bumblebee and Latrodectus campaigns of 2024.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=msiexec.exe Processes.process="*/i*http*" by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id _time | rename Processes.* as * | eval msi_epoch=_time | join type=inner host [| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as rundll_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=rundll32.exe (Processes.process="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\Local\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\ProgramData\\*") Processes.process="*.dll,*" by host Processes.user Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name | rename Processes.* as * | rename process as rundll_cmd | rename user as rundll_user] | where rundll_time>=msi_epoch AND rundll_time<=msi_epoch+1800 | table firstTime host user process rundll_user rundll_cmd parent_process_name | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMin = 30m;
let _legit_msi_parents = dynamic(["servicing\\trustedinstaller.exe","system32\\msiexec.exe","sccm","ccmexec.exe","chocolatey\\choco.exe"]);
let RemoteMsi = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName =~ "msiexec.exe"
    // remote-URL install — TA578 lobs MSIs straight off Cloudflare R2 / Wasabi / Discord CDN
    | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s/[iq]\s+\"?https?://"
    // exclude IT-managed software-install parentage
    | where not(InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_legit_msi_parents))
    // browser / script-host parentage is the TA578 tell — JS contact-form droppers, browser MOTW bypass
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","explorer.exe","javaw.exe","powershell.exe")
    | project MsiTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              MsiParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              MsiParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              MsiCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
// DLL in user-writable path with explicit export name — Latrodectus 'AlexolTon' / 'Run', IcedID 'PluginInit', Bumblebee 'IternalJob'
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)[\"\s]([A-Z]:)?\\(Users\\[^\\]+\\AppData\\(Roaming|Local)|ProgramData)\\[^\"]+\.(dll|tmp|dat|ocx|cpl)[\"\s]?,\s*\w{3,32}"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("msiexec.exe","rundll32.exe")
| join kind=inner RemoteMsi on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (MsiTime .. MsiTime + WindowMin)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, MsiTime)
| project MsiTime, RundllTime = Timestamp, DelaySec,
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          MsiParent, MsiParentCmd, MsiCmd,
          RundllCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          RundllSha256 = SHA256
| order by RundllTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMin = 30m;
let RemoteMsi = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where NewProcessName endswith "\\msiexec.exe"
    | where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s/[iq]\s+\"?https?://"
    | where ParentProcessName has_any ("\\wscript.exe","\\cscript.exe","\\mshta.exe","\\chrome.exe","\\msedge.exe","\\firefox.exe","\\brave.exe","\\explorer.exe","\\javaw.exe","\\powershell.exe")
    | project MsiTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              MsiParent = ParentProcessName, MsiCmd = CommandLine;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\rundll32.exe"
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)[\"\s]([A-Z]:)?\\(Users\\[^\\]+\\AppData\\(Roaming|Local)|ProgramData)\\[^\"]+\.(dll|tmp|dat|ocx|cpl)[\"\s]?,\s*\w{3,32}"
| where ParentProcessName has_any ("\\msiexec.exe","\\rundll32.exe")
| join kind=inner RemoteMsi on Computer
| where TimeGenerated between (MsiTime .. MsiTime + WindowMin)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, MsiTime)
| project MsiTime, RundllTime = TimeGenerated, DelaySec,
          Computer, Account, MsiParent, MsiCmd,
          RundllCmd = CommandLine
| order by RundllTime desc
ucs.rationale: TA578 is the canonical 'contact-form → JS → remote MSI → rundll32 export' actor — Proofpoint, Bitsight, and Microsoft (Storm-0539-adjacent reporting) have repeatedly tied this exact loader topology to TA578 distribution of IcedID, Bumblebee, and Latrodectus. Generic msiexec hunts overfire on legit deployments; what makes this actor-specific is (a) /i with an http(s) URL (TA578 hosts MSIs on Cloudf

`AppData` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Roaming` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Local` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`ProgramData` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `Users` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent. Did you mean `UserSid`?
`AppData` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.

ucs.title: [LLM] TA578 contact-form / DMCA-lure JS dropper: browser-spawned wscript executing JS from user-download paths
ucs.description: TA578's signature initial-access vector is the website contact form abuse and DMCA/copyright-complaint email lure that lands the victim on a Google Sites / Cloudflare page hosting a .js file the user is tricked into running. This UC catches the consequent execution: a browser process (or Outlook) spawning wscript.exe / cscript.exe against a .js / .jse file in Downloads or %Temp%, T1059.007 + T1204.001 chained.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=wscript.exe OR Processes.process_name=cscript.exe) (Processes.parent_process_name=chrome.exe OR Processes.parent_process_name=msedge.exe OR Processes.parent_process_name=firefox.exe OR Processes.parent_process_name=brave.exe OR Processes.parent_process_name=outlook.exe OR Processes.parent_process_name=explorer.exe) (Processes.process="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*") (Processes.process="*.js*" OR Processes.process="*.jse*" OR Processes.process="*.wsf*") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id | rename Processes.* as * | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | table firstTime host user parent_process_name process
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
// browser / mailclient / explorer parent — the TA578 click-and-open tell
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","arc.exe","outlook.exe","explorer.exe")
// JS / JSE / WSF dropped to user-writable land
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\\(Downloads|Temp|AppData\\Local\\Temp)\\[^\\\"]+\.(js|jse|wsf|vbs)\b"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          BrowserParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          BrowserParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ScriptHost = FileName,
          ScriptCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          IsRemoteSession = AdditionalFields
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\wscript.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "\\cscript.exe"
| where ParentProcessName has_any ("\\chrome.exe","\\msedge.exe","\\firefox.exe","\\brave.exe","\\arc.exe","\\outlook.exe","\\explorer.exe")
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\\(Downloads|Temp|AppData\\Local\\Temp)\\[^\\\"]+\.(js|jse|wsf|vbs)\b"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          BrowserParent = ParentProcessName,
          ScriptHost = NewProcessName,
          ScriptCmd = CommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: TA578 Browser-Spawned WScript Executing JS Dropper from User Download Path
id: 6b7e2a18-6d2a-4f3a-9b48-ta578js0001
status: experimental
description: Detects TA578's contact-form / DMCA-lure JavaScript dropper execution — wscript/cscript spawned by a browser or Outlook against a .js / .jse / .wsf in Downloads or Temp.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1038/
  - https://www.proofpoint.com/us/blog/threat-insight/latrodectus-this-spider-bytes-like-ice
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.007
  - attack.t1204.001
  - detection.threat_hunting
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_browser:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\brave.exe'
      - '\arc.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\explorer.exe'
  script_host:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
  script_target:
    CommandLine|re|i: '\\(Downloads|Temp|AppData\\Local\\Temp)\\[^\\\"]+\.(js|jse|wsf|vbs)\b'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: parent_browser and script_host and script_target and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate JScript installers downloaded by IT (rare in modern environments)
level: high
ucs.rationale: TA578 is uniquely identified by initial-access via abuse of public website contact forms and 'stolen images / DMCA' email threads that pivot the victim to a JS-on-Google-Sites lure. The actor's tradecraft has not deviated from the JS-dropper handoff across IcedID → Bumblebee → Latrodectus (MITRE G1038 enumerates exactly T1059.007 + T1204.001 + T1583.006). A wscript/cscript spawned by a browser or
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
